sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं केशवस्य कृताञ्जलिर्वेपमानः किरीटी। नमस्कृत्वा भूय एवाह कृष्णं सगद्गदं भीतभीतः प्रणम्य॥
transliteration: etac chrutvä vacanaà keçavasya kåtäïjalir vepamänaù kirété / namaskåtvä bhüya eväha kåñëaà sa-gadgadaà bhéta-bhétaù praëamya
literal_meaning: Having heard these words of Keśava, Arjuna—hands folded, trembling, diadem-adorned—having bowed again, spoke to Kṛṣṇa with a faltering voice, utterly afraid, prostrating.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s nervous-system shifts from awe to overwhelm; the ego’s familiar reference points dissolve, producing trembling, stammering, and repeated prostration—classic signs of a full-blown existential/numinous encounter.
psychological_state: Acute hyper-arousal and ego-shock; transition from companionate equality to devotional dependence.
trigger_scenario: A high-control CEO suddenly grasps the full human cost of his decisions during a crisis and, voice shaking, asks the team for forgiveness.
anchor_text: When certainty collapses, bow, breathe, and let the trembling teach you.
